title: Measles — Complicated with Pneumonia in Unvaccinated Child

patient_data.chief_complaint: High fever for 5 days with rash spreading from face to body, cough worsening
symptoms: Maculopapular rash — started behind ears, spread to face then trunk and extremities
Koplik spots on buccal mucosa (white spots on red base)
patient_data.history: Unvaccinated (parents refused vaccination). Recent measles outbreak in school. Malnourished (low weight-for-age z-score -2.5).

lab_results.CXR: Bilateral interstitial infiltrates consistent with measles pneumonia

should_detect: Koplik spots — pathognomonic for measles (appear before rash)
Classic triad: cough + conjunctivitis + coryza (the 3 Cs)
Measles pneumonia — leading cause of measles death
Vitamin A supplementation is WHO-recommended treatment
Unvaccinated child in outbreak setting — definitive diagnosis
Contact tracing and post-exposure prophylaxis for contacts
